title: Good King Arthur
content:
Good King Arthur.


When good king Arthur ruled this land,
    He was a goodly king;
He stole three pecks of barley-meal,
    To make a bag-pudding.

A bag-pudding the king did make,
    And stuffed it well with plums:
And in it put great lumps of fat,
    As big as my two thumbs.










The king and queen did eat thereof,
    And noblemen beside;
And what they could not eat that night,
    The queen next morning fried.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Leonard Leslie Brooke delivers a powerful performance in "Good King Arthur"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
